steps.title: Read for the signal
steps.content: The passage says bystanders who witness a helpful act become “more likely to offer help” and can “inspire still others to act.” The blank must mean something like promote or nurture the spread of prosocial behavior — a cascading, positive growth process.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘require’: “Require” means to depend on or make obligatory. The text says kindness can inspire more kindness — not that broader prosocial behavior is a prerequisite for individual acts. This word reverses the direction of causality. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘remember’: “Remember” implies holding a memory. Acts of kindness cannot hold memories; the verb is categorically incompatible with the subject. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘foster’: “Foster” means to encourage and develop growth. It precisely describes how witnessing one act nurtures additional prosocial behavior across the group. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘discourage’: “Discourage” means to hinder or deter. The passage states the opposite — bystanders become more likely to help, not less. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
